title: Puffy German Russet Potato Casserole
description: A rich and snappy German dish featuring russet potatoes, puff pastry, and vegetarian-friendly ingredients, baked to perfection in a clay pot.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced potatoes and chopped onion.
In a small bowl, mix together the minced garlic, salt, and black pepper, then sprinkle over the potato mixture.
Roll out the puff pastry on a floured surface to a large rectangle, about 1/4 inch thick.
Spoon the potato mixture onto one half of the pastry, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese over the potato mixture.
Fold the other half of the pastry over the filling, pressing the edges to seal.
Brush the top of the pastry with the vegetable oil and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
Place the casserole in a clay pot and bake for 45 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and puffed.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 10 minutes before serving.
